title: Joins, Cardinality, Unmatched Rows, and Fanout Control · Relational Lens: Outer Join
summary: Ticket, team, owner, and event data must be combined without losing unassigned work or multiplying effort totals. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql outer join increment in a cardinality-safe support investigation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 6 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. Competency probe: Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities. A defensible solution applies Outer Join, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a cardinality-safe support investigation.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-outer-join — Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events
  GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COUNT(tickets.ticket_id) AS tickets,
       COALESCE(SUM(effort.minutes), 0) AS minutes
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN tickets ON tickets.team_id = teams.team_id
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
GROUP BY teams.team_id, teams.name
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Outer Join evidence · Theory Outer Join
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-outer-join-w7cfqz" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; join relationship, unmatched-row, or fanout evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-outer-join-w7cfqz
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT tickets.ticket_id, COALESCE(effort.minutes, 0) AS minutes
FROM tickets
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
ORDER BY tickets.ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
